title: Query Space, Capability, and Preferences with Fallbacks
summary: Build and verify responsive media queries in inventory environment and input capability adaptation through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain responsive media queries from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply responsive media queries without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Inventory environment and input capability adaptation case 1: viewport width is the only useful responsive condition and hover proves mouse use.
Inventory environment and input capability adaptation case 1: keep essential behavior in the base, then use range, capability, and preference queries only for safe enhancements.
Inventory environment and input capability adaptation case 1: place action labels only inside hover rules because desktops support hover.

steps.title: 03 · Read the inventory environment and input capability adaptation case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 3: Which claim preserves responsive media queries when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: inventory environment and input capability adaptation.
steps.why: Query Space, Capability, and Preferences with Fallbacks makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 3 in inventory environment and input capability adaptation.

content.text: Media queries can test width ranges, orientation, resolution, pointer accuracy, hover capability, contrast, color scheme, and motion preferences. Each query should enhance a working fallback. Capability queries describe the environment but never prove a particular user or device, so essential actions cannot depend on hover, orientation, or a fine pointer. Apply it to inventory environment and input capability adaptation.

Inventory environment and input capability adaptation case 3: place action labels only inside hover rules because desktops support hover.
